resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def maxSubstrings(self, word: str) -> int:
        n = len(word)
        res = 0
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i + 4, n + 1):
                if word[i] == word[j - 1] and word[i] == word[j - 2] and word[i] == word[j - 3] and word[i] == word[j - 4]:
                    res += 1
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses two nested loops to generate all possible substrings of length at least 4. It checks if the first and last characters of the substring are the same, and if so, increments the result. This approach has a high efficiency due to the use of a single loop and a simple condition check.
